summary: Patrick shares an early proof of concept for an embeddable widget feature for Bingo Card Creator during his 30-day development sprint. The widget allows content to be distributed across external sites like teacher blogs. Despite its rough appearance, he sees the strategic value: viral distribution that spreads content at near-zero marginal cost, replacing expensive CPC advertising. The next step is generalizing the widget so non-technical users can deploy it without Rails programming knowledge.
key_points: Embeddable widgets enable viral content distribution at zero marginal cost compared to paid advertising
The proof of concept prioritizes functionality over aesthetics, with design help coming later from a collaborator
The widget strategy deepens relationships with core followers by giving them free, useful content to share
The key engineering challenge is making the widget self-service rather than requiring custom Rails development
money_quotes: I have the artistic ability of paint. Not MSPaint, no, just paint.

It propagates itself, for no marginal cost whatsoever, spreads the content I spent so much time and money creating, and gets me new visitors and eventually customers at a bare fraction of the cost of CPC advertising.
Now if I can just generalize it and make it possible to do without a few hours of Rails programming, I can start printing money hats. Small, modest money hats to start with.
Imagine that spread over a few dozen teacher blogs — it propagates itself, for no marginal cost whatsoever.
key_insight: Embeddable widgets turn your existing customers into a zero-cost distribution channel, replacing expensive paid advertising with viral content that deepens relationships while acquiring new users.
